findings.location: functions.php:597 (captcha form output, triggered by sanitize_settings in setup.php which does not sanitize captcha_challenge_text)
findings.reachability: Reachable by any administrator who can save plugin settings; output rendered on the public-facing login page to all visitors when captcha type is 'builtin'.
findings.justification: At functions.php:597, `$options['captcha_challenge_text']` is concatenated directly into HTML output with no escaping: `'

findings.location: functions.php:1074-1078 (login_head output), sanitize_settings in setup.php does not sanitize design_custom_css
findings.reachability: Requires administrator privileges to set the design_custom_css option; output is rendered inside a public-facing login page <style> block.
findings.justification: The `design_custom_css` option is saved in `sanitize_settings` (setup.php) without any sanitization, and then at functions.php:1075 it is output via `esc_html()` inside a `<style>` block: `esc_html()` does not neutralize CSS syntax characters such as `{}`, allowing an admin to inject arbitrary CSS rules including `expression()` or `url()` data-URI tricks visible to all login-page visitors.
